summary: Implemented a substantial matching/decompilation slice for src/melee/vi/vi1201v2.c, centered on un_80320A40_OnEnter and related data/header fixes. The PR replaces a simple Vec3 at un_80400304 with a structured data blob containing the player spawn Vec3 plus embedded asset/assertion strings, adds archive/scene/model/fog/camera setup logic, introduces local HSD_JObj setter variants needed for matching, and corrects un_804D7028 from u8 to GXColor so erase color handling uses named RGBA fields.
what_changed: Added missing includes for baselib forwards, effects, ft_0C31, lb_00B0, lbarchive, toy/tylist, and GXColor support used by the newly decompiled code.

Changed un_80400304 from a Vec3 into an un_80400304_t struct containing player_spawn plus embedded strings such as "!(jobj->flags & JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION)", "Vi1201v2.dat", "visual1201v2Scene", "TyKoopa.dat", "ToyKoopaModel_TopN_joint", "GmRgStnd.dat", and "standScene".
Updated Player_80032768 call sites to pass &un_80400304.player_spawn instead of the old whole-symbol Vec3 address.
Added static/global state for additional scene resources and objects: un_804D7014, un_804D7020, un_804D7028, un_804D702C, un_804D7030, and un_804D7034.
Corrected un_804D7028 from a byte-like color symbol to GXColor and changed un_803208F0 to call HSD_SetEraseColor(colors->r, colors->g, colors->b, colors->a).

Decompiled un_80320A40_OnEnter, including effect initialization, synchronous effect loading, audio state changes, archive symbol loads, camera object creation, player/demo setup, Koopa/model scene object setup, fog setup, light setup, and final audio restore.

Added local static HSD_JObjSetRotationY_2, HSD_JObjSetScaleX_2/Y_2/Z_2, and HSD_JObjSetTranslateX_2/Y_2/Z_2 helper variants that directly mutate JObj fields and conditionally call ftCo_800C6AFC unless JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT is set.
Changed the header prototype for un_80320A40_OnEnter from UNK_T to void* and exported un_804D7028 as GXColor.
smart_moves: Recognized that un_80400304 was not only a Vec3 but the start of a larger rodata/data blob; modeling it as a struct preserved offsets for both the spawn vector and nearby embedded strings.
Used a named GXColor type for un_804D7028, which made the erase-color call structurally correct and avoided treating the color bytes as an untyped u8 array.

Kept the assertion string address expression in HSD_JObjSetRotationY_2 as (char*) &un_804002F8 + 0x18, matching the observed layout rather than inventing a cleaner but potentially non-matching string reference.
Introduced local setter clones with exact assertion line numbers and direct field writes, a common matching tactic when the original inline/static behavior differs from public baselib helper calls.

Named newly understood fields conservatively around evidence from embedded asset names and usage, such as player_spawn, vi1201v2_dat, visual1201v2_scene, tykoopa_dat, toykoopa_model_topn_joint, gmrgstnd_dat, and stand_scene.
decomp_lessons: If a global initially appears to be a Vec3 but later references access nearby string offsets, re-evaluate it as the first field of a larger structured data blob rather than as isolated symbols.
Embedded asset filenames and scene symbol names are strong evidence for struct field boundaries and semantic field names in vi/demo scene code.
For color globals passed to GX/HSD screen erase APIs, prefer GXColor over u8 arrays when the code consumes r/g/b/a fields.

Matching HSD_JObj setter code may require local variants that reproduce assert behavior, source line numbers, direct field mutation, and the matrix-update condition instead of calling exported setters.
When OnEnter code consumes a void* argument as indexed bytes, changing the prototype from UNK_T to void* and casting locally can improve type clarity without overcommitting to a full struct.
naming_conventions: Unknown globals retained address-based names such as un_804D7010, un_804D7014, un_804D7018, un_804D701C, un_804D7020, un_804D7024, un_804D7028, un_804D702C, un_804D7030, and un_804D7034.
Struct fields derived from embedded strings use lowercase snake_case forms of asset/symbol names: vi1201v2_dat, visual1201v2_scene, tykoopa_dat, toykoopa_model_topn_joint, gmrgstnd_dat, stand_scene.
The decompiled entrypoint keeps the module/address style name un_80320A40_OnEnter.
Local helper clones are suffixed with _2, for example HSD_JObjSetRotationY_2 and HSD_JObjSetTranslateX_2, indicating matching-specific variants rather than canonical baselib APIs.
Function names with known subsystems remain prefixed by their subsystem names, such as Player_SetPlayerId, lbArchive_LoadSymbols, efLib_Init, and lbAudioAx_80024E50.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Modeled contiguous data beginning at un_80400304 as one struct so references like data + 0x40, data + 0x50, data + 0x64, data + 0x70, data + 0x8C, and data + 0x9C line up with embedded asset and symbol strings.
Used a local u8* input = arg pattern for un_80320A40_OnEnter so byte accesses input[0] and input[1] match the caller-provided argument usage.
Inserted PAD_STACK(24), suggesting stack layout/padding was needed for matching.
Recreated JObj setters with explicit __assert calls using jobj.h line numbers 660, 661, 776, 791, 806, 932, 947, and 962.
Preserved conditional ftCo_800C6AFC(jobj) calls only when !(jobj->flags & JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT), matching matrix-update side effects in the setter variants.
For the quaternion assertion string, used an address into the adjacent data blob instead of directly naming the struct field, likely to satisfy generated code or relocation shape.
Used typed scene resource loads via lbArchive_LoadSymbols with SceneDesc, HSD_Joint, and archive globals to reconstruct scene/model initialization order.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one issue comment count in metadata, but no human text excerpt was included, so reviewer intent or discussion cannot be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type un_80400304_t fields in vi1201v2.c.
Search for other vi scene OnEnter functions that use embedded asset string blobs and lbArchive_LoadSymbols offsets.
Look for subsequent work replacing HSD_JObjSet*_2 local helpers with shared inline/static definitions.
Check whether un_804D7014, un_804D7020, un_804D702C, un_804D7030, and un_804D7034 receive semantic names in later PRs.
Compare other uses of GXColor erase-color globals around HSD_SetEraseColor for consistent typing.
